title: uva-ursi glauca
intro: Uva-ursi glauca, also known as bearberry, is a low-growing shrub native to northern parts of Europe, Asia, and North America, characterized by its small white flowers and red berries. The plant's leaves contain arbutin, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which is used to treat urinary tract infections, including cystitis and urethritis. Uva-ursi glauca has been traditionally used to prevent and treat bladder and kidney problems, and its medicinal properties have been attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the urinary tract. Herbal preparations of uva-ursi glauca include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally to alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections and promote overall urinary health.
